summary: Sprint B Lot 2 hotfix retest — fire-and-forget invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']}) approach. Code change confirmed in PaymentsPage.js (lines 187-200) and CoachesPage.js (lines 141-150). TEST 1 STILL FAILS but for a different reason than iter75. The dialog now closes faster (~2.7s vs >4s before, still slightly above the 2s window), BUT the UI does NOT auto-refresh AT ALL — the row's 'Impayé' (revert-payment-{id}) button is never replaced by 'Payé' (mark-paid-{id}). Backend logs prove the main `GET /api/payments` query is NEVER refetched after the POST /api/payments/{id}/revert-to-unpaid; only `GET /api/payments/upcoming?days=7` is refetched. This means `invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']})` only triggers active refetch on the ['payments','upcoming'] observer, not on the parent ['payments'] or sibling ['payments','late']. Tests 2 & 3 not run — environment was unstable on subsequent navigations after Test 1 (page sometimes returned 0 payments / Chargement... state).

tests_executed.Test 1 — Payment revert auto-refresh: FAIL — Payment 957faaf8 (Laura Ngo-Fontaine, Versoix). POST /payments/{id}/revert-to-unpaid returned 200. Dialog closed at ~2.71s. Mark-paid button NEVER appeared (waited 5+ seconds). Backend logs show only /api/payments/upcoming refetched after POST, NOT /api/payments or /api/payments/late. Cleanup did NOT run because UI never showed the mark-paid button — payment 957faaf8 is now in 'late' state in DB and needs manual restore by main agent.
tests_executed.Test 2 — Coach rent revert: NOT EXECUTED — same root cause expected (CoachesPage.js uses identical fire-and-forget invalidateQueries({queryKey:['coaches']}) pattern); environment instability after Test 1 prevented a clean run.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking revert-payment-confirm-btn, the row UI does not auto-refresh. Backend logs confirm GET /api/payments is NEVER fired after the POST revert, so the table keeps showing the old (paid) status with the 'Impayé' button visible indefinitely until manual page refresh. Dialog DOES close (around 2.7s), but the user-visible state-swap requirement (≤2s) is not met because the swap NEVER happens.

integration_issues.issue: queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']}) appears to only refetch the ['payments','upcoming'] observer, NOT the main ['payments'] query (the one that drives the rendered table). This is verified by backend logs: after POST revert-to-unpaid (200 OK), only GET /api/payments/upcoming is observed. Suggested fix: explicitly invalidate each key, OR use queryClient.refetchQueries({queryKey:['payments']}) without await, OR call invalidateQueries 3× (one per concrete key) and ensure all three observers refetch. Another option: use `queryClient.setQueryData(['payments'], (old)=>...)` to optimistically patch the local cache from the mutation response, which would update UI instantly without depending on a network refetch.

action_items: HIGH: PaymentsPage.js revert flow — fire-and-forget invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']}) does NOT cause GET /api/payments to refetch (backend log proven). Replace with: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments'], exact:false, refetchType:'all'})` OR explicit triple call: invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments'], exact:true}); invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments','late']}); invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments','upcoming']}). Better: optimistically setQueryData(['payments'], old => old.map(p => p.id===vars.id ? {...p, status:'late', paid_date:null} : p)) inside onSuccess so the UI updates synchronously.
HIGH: Apply the same fix pattern to CoachesPage.js revertCoachRentMutation (line 144) — most likely same symptom.
MEDIUM: Restore payment 957faaf8-a953-4ba8-a31e-6bef50bc0c36 (Laura Ngo-Fontaine) back to 'paid' state via curl — automated cleanup did not run because the UI never showed the mark-paid button.

TESTING ENV NOTE: The Versoix oct 2025 view (73 paid, 122 late) is the only place to find paid payments quickly. After login the default view is mai 2026 with 0 paid. Switch club to 'Transform Versoix' first, then set month input to 2025-10.
critical_code_review_comments: PaymentsPage.js line 191: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']})` is in place (no await) — this fixes the dialog-close blocking issue from iter75. HOWEVER, empirical backend logs show this only triggers refetch of ['payments','upcoming'] observer, not the parent ['payments'] which drives the rendered table. The list never re-renders.

Hypothesis: in v5 the prefix-match invalidation may treat ['payments'] as 'exact match for the prefix' and only match queries where the first key element is 'payments' AND the queryKey has more elements ('upcoming' subkey wins because it was the most recent observer). Recommend testing with `exact: false, refetchType: 'all'` OR migrating to explicit per-key invalidations.

Alternative recommendation: combine the synchronous `setQueryData` cache patch (instant UI update) with a non-awaited invalidate (eventual server reconciliation). Pattern: setQueryData(['payments'], old => old.map(p=> p.id===vars.id ? response.payment : p)); then invalidateQueries(...). This guarantees ≤100ms UI swap regardless of network.
CoachesPage.js line 144: same pattern, same risk.
Positive: dialog is no longer blocked in pending state (this was the iter75 regression). The fix solves part of the problem but not all of it.
Test data risk: Laura Ngo-Fontaine payment 957faaf8 is now in 'late' state because cleanup branch didn't run. Main agent should restore via direct DB update or curl POST /payments/{id}/mark-paid.
seed_data_creation: Payment 957faaf8-a953-4ba8-a31e-6bef50bc0c36 (Laura Ngo-Fontaine, 90 CHF, 27 mars 2026) is currently in 'late' state — cleanup did NOT run because UI never updated. Main agent must manually restore to 'paid' state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The hotfix from iter75 → iter76 partially solved the issue: dialog now closes around 2.7s (was >4s with await Promise.all). But the UI auto-refresh STILL doesn't work because backend logs prove the main `GET /api/payments` query is never refetched after POST revert (only `/api/payments/upcoming` refetches). Root cause is likely the way TanStack v5 prefix-matches invalidate keys vs the active observers. After main agent's NEXT fix, retest by: 1) login antoine.paucod, 2) /payments + Versoix + 2025-10, 3) click any revert-payment-* button, 4) confirm WITHOUT email, 5) within 2s, dialog must close AND `mark-paid-{id}` btn must appear (verifying the row data refreshed). 6) Tail backend logs to confirm GET /api/payments was actually called after POST revert. Tests 2 & 3 should also be retested — Test 2 likely has same root cause (CoachesPage.js uses same pattern).
rca of the issue: Iter75: blocking await on Promise.all([refetchQueries x3]) kept mutation in pending state >4s. Iter76 fix: switched to non-awaited invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']}) — dialog now closes promptly, BUT empirical evidence (backend access logs) shows that this invalidate only triggers refetch on the ['payments','upcoming'] observer, NOT on the parent ['payments'] observer that renders the table. As a result, the cached `payments` array stays stale in the React component and the row never re-renders to show the new status. Mitigation options: (a) use queryClient.setQueryData to patch the cache synchronously from the mutation's response payload (best UX, instant swap); (b) call invalidateQueries 3× explicitly with each concrete queryKey; (c) use {exact:false, refetchType:'all'} options to force refetch of inactive cached queries too.
